title: hypericum lanceolatum subsp. lanceolatum
intro: Hypericum lanceolatum subsp. lanceolatum is a subspecies of the St. John's Wort plant, commonly known as a flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin, making it a natural remedy for mild to moderate depression and anxiety. The plant's health benefits also extend to its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. Hypericum lanceolatum subsp. lanceolatum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine.
